articles.title: Siri AI Hands On: A Smart, Helpful Assistant
articles.subtitle: The new Siri AI is conversational, omnipresent, and actually helpful.
articles.author: Reece Rogers
articles.summary: The new Siri AI is conversational, omnipresent, and actually helpful.
body: I’m outside hiking and testing a developer beta of Siri AI , Apple’s revamped voice assistant, when fog engulfs the Golden Gate Bridge behind me. It’s beautiful, but I’m coatless, cold, and ready for something cozy. So, I pull out my iPhone and ask this new Siri where I can grab some fluffy pancakes nearby. A translucent orb at the top of the smartphone screen spins around a few times, then the voice assistant responds with a recommendation: a spot called Eats in the Inner Richmond.

This version of Siri—conversational, omnipresent, actually helpful—has been long delayed. At its annual developer conference this year, though, Apple shared how it would finally come to fruition as part of iOS 27 . The voice assistant is now highly personalized based on your messages, photos, and emails. It’s also better at understanding questions and interacting with apps. Basically, it feels nothing like Siri of yore . That’s a good thing.

Since Apple plans to roll out this voice assistant to the public later this year, I was curious to see how helpful Siri AI could be as a travel guide, even in its beta form. How would it feel to spend a day as a Siri-assisted tourist? So I grabbed my iPhone and headed out to explore San Francisco.

The evolution of Siri is striking, even in this nascent iteration. Whereas before Siri was a more limited, isolated experience, it’s now merged into the iPhone search bar and pops up if you swipe down in the middle of your screen. You can chat conversationally with it or swipe down on Siri’s answers to text any follow-up questions. These back-and-forths are stored in a dedicated app, so you can return to past conversations.

I quickly found that Siri AI’s bite-sized replies don’t drone on endlessly like many contemporary AI assistants, often sticking to a single paragraph. When I verbally asked for a nice beach hike route to see the sunrise near the Golden Gate Bridge, it succinctly recommended a popular trail in the Presidio neighborhood as well as an option in the Marin Headlands. Siri bolded key words in the text answer that appeared alongside the audible response for easy scanning. Since I wanted more info before heading out, I swiped down on the text answer to read additional details about each option.

Apple’s partnership with Google is a core driver behind this Siri overhaul. Google’s Gemini now helps power the voice assistant’s underlying model, Apple Intelligence . Siri’s output with this new model felt more attuned to what I was looking for, rather than just suggesting a couple of website links for me to dig through. When I asked generic questions, like “What should I do today,” Siri combed through my recent messages and highlighted recent plans I started discussing with friends but never finalized.

Another key aspect of Siri AI is hyper-personalization based on what you have on your device, whether that data is in your photos or messages. It also doesn’t keep you locked into Apple-only services; when I asked Siri to draft a text, the voice assistant confirmed if I wanted to send it through Apple’s Messages or Meta’s Messenger service.

This style of AI search requires Siri to index your phone, which means scanning and cataloging its data for easy reference. When I updated my iPhone to the developer beta for iOS 27, it took a little over a week for the device to fully index.

At WWDC 2026, Apple repeatedly referenced its privacy-preserving approach to Siri AI. As part of the company’s Private Cloud Compute , Apple claims it doesn’t store data from users and only pulls from it when you ask Siri a question. Similar to the previous version of Apple’s assistant, users who aren’t interested can turn off Siri AI in their settings.

I tested Siri AI on an iPhone 16 Pro Max , which will have many but not all of Siri AI’s features. Based on what’s been publicly released, only the iPhone Air, iPhone 17 Pro, and the iPhone 17 Max will have all the fixings, like more varied voice options. As for the rest of the lineup: Every iPhone 16 and iPhone 17 model will be able to run the new Siri, while only the iPhone 15 Pro and Pro Max will be compatible. Older models will not support this voice assistant.

Like any good tourist, I started my morning off with a visit to the Golden Gate Bridge, where there are plenty of nearby hiking trails to wander around and soak up the views. Since Siri AI is also integrated with the iPhone’s camera app , I decided to open it up and snap a quick photo of the foggy path ahead of me to see how Siri would respond.

I didn’t ask Siri any questions; I just showed it a snapshot of what I saw at the moment, and the AI tool responded with a short history of the Cypress Tree Tunnel located at Point Reyes National Seashore. Siri was right to clock the Monterey cypress trees. But the tunnel it referenced is an hour drive from where I was, potentially confusing for someone not familiar with the area.
keyPoints: I’m outside hiking and testing a developer beta of Siri AI , Apple’s revamped voice assistant, when fog engulfs the Golden Gate Bridge behind me. It’s beautiful, but I’m coatless, cold, and ready for something cozy. So, I pull out my iPhone and ask this new Siri where I can grab some fluffy pancakes nearby. A translucent orb at the top of the smartphone screen spins around a few times, then the voice assistant responds with a recommendation: a spot called Eats in the Inner Richmond.

This version of Siri—conversational, omnipresent, actually helpful—has been long delayed. At its annual developer conference this year, though, Apple shared how it would finally come to fruition as part of iOS 27 . The voice assistant is now highly personalized based on your messages, photos, and emails. It’s also better at understanding questions and interacting with apps. Basically, it feels nothing like Siri of yore . That’s a good thing.

Since Apple plans to roll out this voice assistant to the public later this year, I was curious to see how helpful Siri AI could be as a travel guide, even in its beta form. How would it feel to spend a day as a Siri-assisted tourist? So I grabbed my iPhone and headed out to explore San Francisco.
paragraphTranslations: 我在外面徒步旅行，测试苹果公司重新设计的语音助手Siri AI的开发者测试版，这时雾气笼罩了我身后的金门大桥。景色很美，但我没有穿外套，感觉冷，想要找个温暖舒适的地方。于是，我拿出我的iPhone，问这个新的Siri附近哪里可以吃到蓬松的煎饼。智能手机屏幕顶部的一个半透明球体转了几圈，然后语音助手回应了一个建议：一个叫做Eats的地方，在里士满内部区域。
这个版本的Siri——会话式的、无所不在的、真正有用的——已经推迟了很久。然而在今年的年度开发者大会上，苹果公司分享了它将如何最终实现，作为iOS 27的一部分。现在的语音助手根据你的消息、照片和电子邮件高度个性化。它也更擅长理解问题和与应用程序交互。基本上，它感觉一点也不像以前的Siri。这是一件好事。
由于苹果计划在今年晚些时候将这个语音助手推向公众，我很好奇Siri AI作为旅行指南能有多有帮助，即使是在其测试版中。作为一个Siri辅助的游客度过一天会是什么感觉？于是我拿起我的iPhone，出发去探索旧金山。
即使是在这个初期版本中，Siri的演变也是引人注目的。以前Siri是一个更有限、更孤立的体验，现在它已经融入了iPhone的搜索栏，如果你在屏幕中间向下滑动，它就会弹出。你可以与它进行会话式的聊天，或者在Siri的回答上向下滑动以发送任何后续问题。这些来回的对话存储在一个专门的应用程序中，所以你可以随时回顾过去的对话。
我很快发现Siri AI的简短回复不会像许多当代AI助手那样无休止地嗡嗡作响，通常只坚持一个段落。当我口头询问一个在金门大桥附近看日出的好海滩徒步路线时，它简洁地推荐了一个在Presidio社区的热门小径以及一个在马林海角的选择。Siri在文本回答中加粗了关键词，这些关键词出现在可听见的回答旁边，便于扫描。由于我想在出发前了解更多信息，我向下滑动了文本答案，阅读了每个选项的更多细节。
苹果与谷歌的合作是这次Siri大修的核心驱动力。谷歌的Gemini现在帮助驱动语音助手的基础模型，苹果智能。使用这个新模型的Siri输出感觉更符合我要找的内容，而不是仅仅建议几个网站链接让我去挖掘。当我提出通用问题时，比如“我今天应该做什么”，Siri会浏览我最近的信息，并突出显示我最近开始与朋友讨论但尚未最终确定的计划。
Siri AI的另一个关键方面是基于你的设备上的内容进行超个性化定制，无论是你的照片还是消息中的数据。它也不会让你锁定在仅限苹果的服务中；当我问Siri起草一条文本时，语音助手确认我是否想通过苹果的消息或Meta的Messenger服务发送。
这种风格的AI搜索需要Siri索引你的手机，这意味着扫描和编目其数据以便于参考。当我将我的iPhone更新为iOS 27的开发者测试版时，设备花了一周多一点的时间才能完全索引。
在2026年的WWDC上，苹果公司反复提到了其保护隐私的Siri AI方法。作为公司私有云计算的一部分，苹果声称它不存储用户数据，只有在你向Siri提问时才会从中提取。与苹果助手的上一版本类似，不感兴趣的用户可以在他们的设置中关闭Siri AI。

我在iPhone 16 Pro Max上测试了Siri AI，这款手机将拥有许多但不是全部的Siri AI功能。根据已经公开发布的信息，只有iPhone Air、iPhone 17 Pro和iPhone 17 Max将拥有全部功能，如更多样化的声音选项。至于其他机型：每款iPhone 16和iPhone 17型号都将能够运行新的Siri，而只有iPhone 15 Pro和Pro Max将兼容。旧型号将不支持这个语音助手。
像任何好的游客一样，我早上首先参观了金门大桥，那里附近有很多徒步小径可以四处漫步，欣赏风景。由于Siri AI也集成在iPhone的相机应用中，我决定打开它，快速拍一张我面前雾蒙蒙的小径的照片，看看Siri会如何回应。
我没有向Siri提出任何问题；我只是向它展示了我当时看到的快照，AI工具回应了位于雷耶斯国家海岸的柏树隧道的简短历史。Siri正确地识别了蒙特雷柏树。但它提到的隧道距离我所在的地方有一个小时的车程，对于不熟悉该地区的人来说可能会感到困惑。

paragraphs: I tested Siri AI, Apple's improved voice assistant, while hiking. It recommended a place for pancakes and helped with travel plans. The new Siri is more personalized and better at understanding questions. It works with iPhone's search bar and can interact with apps. Siri AI is powered by Google's Gemini and is personalized based on your device's data. It respects privacy and can be turned off if not wanted. I tried Siri AI on an iPhone 16 Pro Max, but only newer models will have all its features.

articles.title: Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
articles.subtitle: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He ha
articles.author: Jessica Hamzelou
articles.summary: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He has now spent almost three years using a brain-computer interface (BCI) that enables…
body: The number of people with electrodes in their brains is believed to have more than doubled in the last couple of years.

This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell —a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He has now spent almost three years using a brain-computer interface (BCI) that enables him to “speak,” surf the web, and perform his job as a climate activist, largely independently.

Since Harrell was implanted with the device, in July 2023, a team at the University of California, Davis, has worked with him to adjust and improve its offerings. They’ve refined its accuracy, for example. And they’ve introduced settings including a privacy mode and a “profanity filter” that lets Harrell talk to his daughter without risking accidental swearing.
Harrell told me that, for him, the device is “nothing short of revolutionary!” It has enabled him to maintain an income, reconnect with friends and family, and read to his daughter.

The team that developed his BCI is one of several working on ways to use technology to allow people with paralysis to communicate, engage with the online world, and regain some independence. And Harrell is one of a growing number of people volunteering their brains to, as he puts it, “pay it forward and do the scientific research … [and] get some personal benefit.”

Over the past couple of years, the number of BCI trial volunteers has soared. This year, China became the first country to approve a BCI for medical use. Advances in technology are allowing engineers to provide more features than ever. BCI research is properly taking off.

I should first point out that BCIs come in different forms . Harrell’s device includes a set of electrodes embedded in his brain that pick up the electrical activity associated with speech. Those electrodes are connected to two docking ports on top of his head that can be plugged into a computer.

That computer is loaded with software trained to decode his brain signals into phonemes (units of sound in speech) and predict what Harrell wants to say. He can then use an eye gaze tracker to make any corrections before the speech is played out loud.

But some BCIs don’t need to be “plugged in”—they’re fully implanted and wireless. Others are less invasive; they might involve placing wired electrodes on the surface of the brain or simply wearing a cap of electrodes, for example. There are trade-offs—the closer you get to the neurons you want to record from, the better your signal will be. But generally speaking, the more invasive the surgery, the higher the risk of complications.

BCIs can also have different functions. Harrell has ALS, but most BCIs in use today are sitting in the brains of people with spinal cord injuries. Typically, these individuals have some degree of paralysis; for example, they may be unable to move their arms and legs, but their face and ability to speak are unaffected. In those cases, BCIs can be used to control other kinds of devices that might help with mobility.

In 2024, Michelle Patrick-Krueger, then at the University of Houston, and her colleagues published a roundup of all trials of BCIs conducted between 1998 , which is when they believe the first device was implanted, and the end of 2023. They identified 21 research groups that, among them, had trialed BCIs in a total of 67 volunteers.

“Since then, that number has increased a lot,” says Mariska Vansteensel, a BCI researcher at University Medical Center Utrecht. In January, Neuralink (the BCI company founded by trillionaire Elon Musk) announced that it has implanted 21 people with its device in the past two years.
keyPoints: The number of people with electrodes in their brains is believed to have more than doubled in the last couple of years.

This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell —a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He has now spent almost three years using a brain-computer interface (BCI) that enables him to “speak,” surf the web, and perform his job as a climate activist, largely independently.

Since Harrell was implanted with the device, in July 2023, a team at the University of California, Davis, has worked with him to adjust and improve its offerings. They’ve refined its accuracy, for example. And they’ve introduced settings including a privacy mode and a “profanity filter” that lets Harrell talk to his daughter without risking accidental swearing.
paragraphTranslations: 过去几年中，大脑中植入电极的人数被认为已经翻了一番多。
本周，我报道了凯西·哈雷尔的故事——他患有肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS），据与他合作的研究人员称，他是“第一个使用大脑植入物的高级用户”。哈雷尔瘫痪了，没有这个设备就无法清晰地说话。他现在已经使用了近三年的脑机接口（BCI），使他能够“说话”、上网冲浪，并主要独立地执行他作为气候活动家的工作。
自从哈雷尔在2023年7月植入了这个设备以来，加州大学戴维斯分校的一个团队一直与他合作调整和改进其功能。例如，他们提高了其准确性。他们还引入了包括隐私模式和“亵渎过滤器”在内的设置，让哈雷尔在与女儿交谈时不会意外地说出脏话。
哈雷尔告诉我，对他来说，这个设备是“革命性的！”它使他能够保持收入，与朋友和家人重新建立联系，并给女儿读书。
开发他的BCI的团队是几个正在研究如何利用技术让瘫痪的人交流、参与在线世界并重获一些独立性的团队之一。哈雷尔是越来越多自愿将大脑用于“向前推进并进行科学研究……[并]获得一些个人利益”的人之一。
在过去的几年里，BCI试验志愿者的数量激增。今年，中国成为第一个批准BCI用于医疗用途的国家。技术的进步使工程师能够提供比以往更多的功能。BCI研究正在真正起飞。
我首先应该指出，BCI有不同形式。哈雷尔的设备包括一组嵌入在他大脑中的电极，这些电极可以接收与言语相关的电活动。这些电极连接到他头顶上的两个对接端口，可以插入计算机。
那台计算机装有经过训练的软件，可以将他的大脑信号解码成音素（言语中的声音单位），并预测哈雷尔想要说什么。然后他可以使用眼动追踪器在语音大声播放之前进行任何更正。
但有些BCI不需要‘插入’——它们是完全植入的并且是无线的。其他的侵入性较小；它们可能涉及将有线电极放置在大脑表面或简单地戴上一顶电极帽，例如。有权衡——你越接近你想要记录的神经元，你的信号就会越好。但一般来说，手术越侵入性，并发症的风险就越高。
BCI也可以有不同的功能。哈雷尔患有ALS，但今天使用的大多数BCI都植入在脊髓损伤的人的大脑中。通常情况下，这些人有一定程度的瘫痪；例如，他们可能无法移动手臂和腿，但他们的面部和说话能力不受影响。在这些情况下，BCI可以用来控制其他可能有助于移动性的设备。
在2024年，当时在休斯顿大学的米歇尔·帕特里克-克鲁格和她同事们发表了一份综述，总结了从1998年到2023年底进行的所有BCI试验，他们认为第一个设备是在1998年植入的。他们确定了21个研究小组，他们总共在67名志愿者中试验了BCI。
“从那时起，这个数字增加了很多，”乌得勒支大学医学中心的BCI研究员马里斯卡·范·斯特恩塞尔说。今年1月，由亿万富翁埃隆·马斯克创立的BCI公司Neuralink宣布，在过去两年中，它已经在21人中植入了其设备。

paragraphs: The number of people with brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) has more than doubled in recent years. Casey Harrell, a man with ALS, has been using a BCI for nearly three years, which allows him to communicate, surf the web, and work as a climate activist. Researchers at the University of California, Davis, have improved the device's accuracy and added features like privacy mode and a profanity filter. BCI research is growing, with China recently approving a BCI for medical use. There are different types of BCIs, from fully implanted wireless devices to less invasive ones that involve wearing a cap of electrodes. BCIs are primarily used by people with spinal cord injuries to help with mobility.
paragraphTranslations: 近年来，使用脑机接口（BCI）的人数增加了一倍多。患有肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）的凯西·哈雷尔已经使用BCI近三年，这使他能够进行交流、上网冲浪和作为气候活动家工作。加州大学戴维斯分校的研究人员提高了设备的准确性，并增加了隐私模式和亵渎过滤器等功能。BCI研究正在增长，中国最近批准了BCI用于医疗用途。BCI有不同类型，从完全植入的无线设备到不那么侵入性的设备，比如戴一顶电极帽。BCI主要用于脊髓损伤的人，以帮助他们移动。

vocabulary.exampleZh: 电极接收与语言相关的电活动。

vocabulary.example: They've refined its accuracy.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 他们提高了它的准确性。

vocabulary.example: They've introduced a 'profanity filter'.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 他们引入了一个‘亵渎过滤器’。

vocabulary.example: They might involve placing wired electrodes on the surface of the brain or simply wearing a cap of electrodes.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 可能涉及在大脑表面放置有线电极或简单地戴上一顶电极帽。

vocabulary.example: BCIs can be used to control other kinds of devices that might help with mobility.
vocabulary.exampleZh: BCI可以用来控制其他可能有助于移动的设备。

vocabulary.example: They published a roundup of all trials of BCIs.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 他们发表了所有BCI试验的综述。

paragraphs: The number of people with brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) has more than doubled in recent years. Casey Harrell, a man with ALS, has been using a BCI for nearly three years, which allows him to communicate, surf the web, and work as a climate activist. Researchers at the University of California, Davis, have improved the device's accuracy and added features like privacy mode and a profanity filter. BCI research is growing, with China recently approving a BCI for medical use. There are different types of BCIs, from fully implanted wireless devices to less invasive ones that involve wearing a cap of electrodes. BCIs are primarily used by people with spinal cord injuries to help with mobility.
paragraphTranslations: 近年来，使用脑机接口（BCI）的人数增加了一倍多。患有肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）的凯西·哈雷尔已经使用BCI近三年，这使他能够进行交流、上网冲浪和作为气候活动家工作。加州大学戴维斯分校的研究人员提高了设备的准确性，并增加了隐私模式和亵渎过滤器等功能。BCI研究正在增长，中国最近批准了BCI用于医疗用途。BCI有不同类型，从完全植入的无线设备到不那么侵入性的设备，比如戴一顶电极帽。BCI主要用于脊髓损伤的人，以帮助他们移动。
